message.content:
# KB Page: npm package `qs`

## Overview

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Registry** | npm |
| **Package** | [qs](https://www.npmjs.com/package/qs) |
| **Latest Version** | 6.15.1 |
| **Weekly Downloads** | ~143,160,104 (week of 2026-04-06 to 2026-04-12) |
| **Repository** | [https://github.com/ljharb/qs](https://github.com/ljharb/qs) |
| **Current Status** | Actively maintained |

## Security Contact / Disclosure Policy

The repository uses **GitHub Security Advisories** for coordinated disclosure, as evidenced by multiple advisories published under `github.com/ljharb/qs/security/advisories/`. No separate security contact email or dedicated security policy document was identified in the gathered evidence.

## Audit History & Known Vulnerabilities

Six publicly disclosed vulnerabilities have been identified in the evidence:

| CVE / GHSA | Summary | Severity Context | Affected Versions | Fixed In | Published |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **CVE-2014-7191** / GHSA-jjv7-qpx3-h62q | DoS via memory exhaustion from crafted large sparse arrays | DoS | < 1.0.0 | 1.0.0 | 2017-10-24 |
| **CVE-2014-10064** / GHSA-f9cm-p3w6-xvr3 | DoS via extended event loop blocking from deeply nested JSON string | DoS | < 1.0.0 | 1.0.0 | 2018-10-09 |
| **CVE-2017-1000048** / GHSA-gqgv-6jq5-jjj9 | Prototype pollution protection bypass via `[` or `]` in input | Prototype Pollution / potential RCE | < 6.0.4, 6.1.x < 6.1.2, 6.2.x < 6.2.3, 6.3.x < 6.3.2 | 6.0.4, 6.1.2, 6.2.3, 6.3.2 | 2020-04-30 |
| **CVE-2022-24999** / GHSA-hrpp-h998-j3pp | Prototype pollution via `__proto__` key causing Node process hang | Prototype Pollution / DoS | < 6.10.3 (backported to 6.2.4–6.9.7) | 6.10.3 (and backports: 6.9.7, 6.8.3, 6.7.3, 6.6.1, 6.5.3, 6.4.1, 6.3.3, 6.2.4) | 2022-11-27 |
| **CVE-2025-15284** / GHSA-6rw7-vpxm-498p | `arrayLimit` bypass in bracket notation (`a[]=`) allows DoS via memory exhaustion; severity reduced because default `parameterLimit` (1000) mitigates the worst-case scenario | DoS (consistency bug) | < 6.14.1 | 6.14.1 | 2025-12-30 |
| **CVE-2026-2391** / GHSA-w7fw-mjwx-w883 | `arrayLimit` bypass in comma parsing (`comma: true`) allows DoS via memory exhaustion from a single parameter with many commas; bypass similar to CVE-2025-15284 | DoS | < 6.14.2 | 6.14.2 | 2026-02-12 |

## Security Posture Notes

- **Long track record of vulnerabilities and fixes.** The package has addressed security issues consistently since 2014, with the maintainer (`ljharb`) issuing fixes and backports across multiple release lines.
- **Recurring attack surface: array/object parsing limits.** Three of the six vulnerabilities (CVE-2014-7191, CVE-2025-15284, CVE-2026-2391) involve DoS through array size or memory exhaustion in parsing logic. The `arrayLimit` enforcement has required successive patches (bracket notation in 6.14.1, comma parsing in 6.14.2).
- **Prototype pollution addressed twice** (CVE-2017-1000048, CVE-2022-24999). The 2022 fix was backported to eight release lines, indicating awareness of the wide deployment surface.
- **Defense-in-depth options added.** Per changelog evidence, version 6.14.0 added `throwOnParameterLimitExceeded`, and 6.13.0 added `strictDepth`, indicating proactive hardening of parsing limits.
- **Default `parameterLimit` (1000)
